clinical_rationale: 62F, 18 months post sigmoid colectomy for adenocarcinoma Stage IIB. 3-month surveillance showing CEA rising over 3 consecutive measurements (2.1 → 4.8 → 9.3 ng/mL). CT abdomen/pelvis — one sub-centimetre peritoneal deposit of uncertain significance in the pouch of Douglas. ECOG 1 (mild fatigue). Not meeting RECIST progression criteria on current scan, but the CEA trajectory and new finding are concerning. Oncology MDT discussion recommended within 2 weeks.
recommended_actions: Urgent oncology MDT review — suspicious peritoneal deposit + rising CEA
Repeat CT chest/abdomen/pelvis in 6 weeks (not 6 months)
Consider PET-CT to characterise peritoneal deposit

sbar.situation: AMBER: 62F — 18 months post sigmoid colectomy for Stage IIB colon adenocarcinoma. CEA rising over 3 consecutive readings; query peritoneal deposit on CT. Oncology MDT review recommended within 2 weeks.
sbar.background: 62-year-old female underwent laparoscopic sigmoid colectomy Jan 2024 for pT3N0M0 adenocarcinoma (Stage IIB). No adjuvant chemotherapy (MSI-H tumour). Serial surveillance per NCCN guidelines. Previous 3 CEA values: 2.1 (Aug), 4.8 (Nov), 9.3 (Feb). CT report notes 8mm soft tissue deposit in pouch of Douglas — 'uncertain, NCRM'. ECOG 1.
sbar.assessment: Rising CEA over 3 measurements (doubling time ~90 days) is a recognised early indicator of recurrence (sensitivity 79%, specificity 70% at this threshold). The sub-centimetre peritoneal deposit, while not definitively RECIST progression, adds clinical concern. Patient reports new fatigue (ECOG upgraded 0→1). No other localising symptoms.
sbar.recommendation: Convene oncology MDT within 2 weeks to review imaging and biomarkers. Arrange PET-CT to characterise peritoneal focus. Repeat CEA in 4 weeks. Repeat CT in 6 weeks if PET equivocal. Patient counselled on findings and given dedicated oncology nurse contact. Ensure colonoscopy (1-year) is confirmed.
